---
title: "pml_writeup"
author: "Chaithra"
date: "3/9/2026"
output: html_document
---
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, cache = TRUE,
                      warning = FALSE, message = FALSE,
                      fig.width = 9, fig.height = 6)

library(caret)
library(rpart)
library(rpart.plot)
library(randomForest)
library(gbm)

# Important: tell read.csv that #DIV/0! and empty strings are NA
training <- read.csv("pml-training.csv",
                     na.strings = c("NA", "", "#DIV/0!"),
                     stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

testing <- read.csv("pml-testing.csv",
                    na.strings = c("NA", "", "#DIV/0!"),
                    stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Convert outcome to factor **early**
training$classe <- factor(training$classe)

# Remove identifier / timestamp / window columns
cols_to_drop <- c("X", "user_name", "raw_timestamp_part_1", "raw_timestamp_part_2",
                  "cvtd_timestamp", "new_window", "num_window")

training <- training[, !names(training) %in% cols_to_drop]
testing  <- testing[,  !names(testing)  %in% cols_to_drop]

# Near-zero variance removal
nzv_idx <- nearZeroVar(training)
training <- training[, -nzv_idx]
testing  <- testing[, -nzv_idx]

# Remove columns that are almost all NA (> 95%)
mostly_na <- colMeans(is.na(training)) > 0.95
training <- training[, !mostly_na]
testing  <- testing[, !mostly_na]

dim(training)   # should be ~ 19622 rows × ~ 53 cols + classe

set.seed(2025)

inTrain <- createDataPartition(training$classe, p = 0.70, list = FALSE)
trainSet <- training[inTrain, ]
validSet <- training[-inTrain, ]

# Make sure classe is factor in both sets 
trainSet$classe  <- factor(trainSet$classe)
validSet$classe  <- factor(validSet$classe)

set.seed(2025)

tree_model <- rpart(classe ~ ., data = trainSet, method = "class")

rpart.plot(tree_model, main = "Decision Tree", extra = 101, under = TRUE)

pred_tree <- predict(tree_model, validSet, type = "class")

# Ensure both are factors with same levels
pred_tree  <- factor(pred_tree,  levels = levels(validSet$classe))

confusionMatrix(pred_tree, validSet$classe)

## Random Forest – fast version

set.seed(2025)

rf_model <- randomForest(
  classe ~ .,
  data       = trainSet,
  ntree      = 120,
  importance = TRUE
)

print(rf_model)

pred_rf <- predict(rf_model, validSet)
pred_rf <- factor(pred_rf, levels = levels(validSet$classe))

confusionMatrix(pred_rf, validSet$classe)

# Variable importance plot
varImpPlot(rf_model, main = "Top variables - Random Forest")

# Test set predictions 
pred_test <- predict(rf_model, testing)
data.frame(problem_id = 1:20, classe = pred_test)

```
